figure.title: Clock Domains in Data Link and Phy
figure.purpose: Shows the clock domain crossings between the Transaction Layer, Data Link Layer, and Physical Layer.

technical_summary: Transmitter Node: The Transaction Layer, Transmit Pacing, and Tx FIFO operate in the UPLI CLK domain (yellow). Flit Packing and the Phy operate in the Transmit CLK domain (blue).
Receiver Node: The Phy and Flit Unpacking operate in the Recovered CLK domain (blue). The Rx FIFO and Transaction Layer operate in the UPLI CLK domain (yellow).
The diagram highlights where data must cross clock boundaries, typically at FIFOs (Tx FIFO and Rx FIFO).

components.name: Data Link
components.description: Bridges the UPLI CLK and Physical layer clocks using FIFOs.

components.name: Phy
components.description: Operates in Transmit CLK (Tx) or Recovered CLK (Rx) domains.

critical_facts: The Data Link layer is responsible for managing clock domain crossings between the synchronous UPLI clock and the potentially asynchronous physical layer clocks using Tx and Rx FIFOs.
